zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Burgh於1290年出生在英國北愛爾蘭庫迪 泰龍時，她的父親Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster31歲，母親Margaret Guines31歲。她於1312年8月16日與Thomas Fitz John Fitz Gerald 2nd Earl of Kildare在愛爾蘭庫迪 唐格林卡斯特爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1359年4月23日在愛爾蘭庫迪 吉爾達吉爾達享年69歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭庫迪 吉爾達吉爾達。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Burgh于1290年出生在英国北爱尔兰庫迪 泰龍时，她的父亲Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster31岁，母亲Margaret Guines31岁。她于1312年8月16日与Thomas Fitz John Fitz Gerald 2nd Earl of Kildare在爱尔兰庫迪 唐格林卡斯特爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1359年4月23日在爱尔兰庫迪 吉爾達吉爾達享年69岁，埋葬在爱尔兰庫迪 吉爾達吉爾達。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).
German: variant of   Burk  .

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
South German: variant of   Buri  .

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Burgh was born in 1290, in County Tyrone, Northern Ireland, United Kingdom, her father, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, was 31 and her mother, Margaret Guines, was 31. She married Thomas Fitz John Fitz Gerald 2nd Earl of Kildare on 16 August 1312, in Greencastle, County Down, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 23 April 1359, in Kildare, County Kildare, Ireland, at the age of 69, and was buried in Kildare, County Kildare, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Burgh est née en 1290, à County Tyrone, Irlande du Nord, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Margaret Guines, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Fitz John Fitz Gerald 2nd Earl of Kildare le 16 août 1312, à Greencastle, County Down, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 23 avril 1359, à Kildare, County Kildare, Irlande, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterrée à Kildare, County Kildare, Irlande.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Burghが1290年にイギリス北アイルランドコウントイ トイロンで生まれた時，父Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulsterは31歳，母Margaret Guinesは31歳であった。1312年8月16日にアイルランドコウントイ ダウングレエンカストルでThomas Fitz John Fitz Gerald 2nd Earl of Kildareと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1359年4月23日にアイルランドコウントイ キルダアキルダアで69歳で死去し，アイルランドコウントイ キルダアキルダアにて埋葬された。

ko.at Athassel Priory, near Cashel, County: 아일랜드 카운티 프 테 크로스 프 티페라리 앳하셀압브이
ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Burgh(이)가 1290년 영국 북아일랜드 카운티 타이론에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Guines(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1312년 8월 16일 아일랜드 카운티 다운 그린카스틀에서 Thomas Fitz John Fitz Gerald 2nd Earl of Kildare(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1359년 4월 23일 아일랜드 카운티 킬더르 킬더르에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드 카운티 킬더르 킬더르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Burgh nasceu em 1290, em County Tyrone, Irlanda do Norte, United Kingdom, seu pai, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Guines, tinha 31 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Fitz John Fitz Gerald 2nd Earl of Kildare em 16 de agosto de 1312, em Greencastle, County Down, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 23 de abril de 1359, em Kildare, County Kildare, Irlanda, com 69 anos, e foi sepultada em Kildare, County Kildare, Irlanda.

ru.at Athassel Priory, near Cashel, County: атасселабби, коунти оф т кросс оф типпирэри, Ирландия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Burgh родилась в 1290 г. в местности коунти тироун, Северная Ирландия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Margaret Guines, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Fitz John Fitz Gerald 2nd Earl of Kildare 16 августа 1312 г. в местности гринкастл, коунти доун, Ирландия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 23 апреля 1359 г. в местности каилдар, коунти каилдар, Ирландия в возрасте 69 лет и была похоронена в местности каилдар, коунти каилдар, Ирландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Burgh nació en 1290, en County Tyrone, Irlanda del Norte, Reino Unido, su papá, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Margaret Guines, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Thomas Fitz John Fitz Gerald 2nd Earl of Kildare el 16 de agosto de 1312, en Greencastle, County Down, Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 23 de abril de 1359, en Kildare, County Kildare, Irlanda, a la edad de 69 años, y fue sepultada en Kildare, County Kildare, Irlanda.
